summary: Epic second set with Truckin', Estimated Prophet and China > Rider
blurb: The July 14 1985 show delivers a marathon second set that roars through Truckin' into a seamless Smokestack Lightning, an inspired Estimated Prophet jam and a scorching China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > Samson sequence. Jerry’s guitar tone is pristine, Phil’s bass shines, and the drums lock in with perfect separation. A mint‑quality soundboard capture makes this one of the season’s must‑listen performances.
review: Ventura County Fairgrounds on July 14, 1985 was a high‑water mark for the Dead’s mid‑80s run. The first set built solid momentum with Hell In A Bucket and an exuberant My Brother Esau, but it was the second set that truly ignited the night—Truckin' surged into a flawless Smokestack Lightning transition, then rolled into a fiery Estimated Prophet that spilled over into an expansive Eyes Of The World. The highlight reel continued with a magical China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > Samson & Delilah medley and a soaring Ship Of Fools before the band rode the groove through Truckin', Black Peter, Throwing Stones and a rousing Not Fade Away finale. Jerry’s guitar tone was described as “perfect” and “soaring,” Phil’s bass remained crystal‑clear with no clipping, and both Mickey and Bill delivered tight, powerful drumming that the mint soundboard captures in pristine detail. Brent’s keyboards sit comfortably in the mix, supporting the fireworks without stealing the spotlight. Whether you listen to the acclaimed Clugston SBD or one of the excellent audience tapes, this show stands out as a quintessential 1985 performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are flagged as positive, repeatedly praising the epic second set, rare So What tease, seamless Truckin' > Smokestack Lightning transition and crystal‑clear soundboard mix. No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of a stellar performance across the board.

best_recording.reason: Mint‑quality soundboard with perfect drum separation, clear bass and guitar tone; captures the legendary second‑set marathon in pristine detail.

key_highlights: Epic Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning transition that propels the second set.
Estimated Prophet jam that flows into an expansive Eyes Of The World.
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > Samson & Delilah sequence shines with tight interplay.
Mint soundboard recording provides crystal‑clear instrument separation.

band_performance.Jerry: His guitar tone is perfect and soaring, carrying the melodic lead on Samson/Space and throughout the marathon second set.
band_performance.Phil: Bass comes in clear and strong with no clipping, providing a solid foundation that shines especially during the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal intensity peaks on Hell In A Bucket; his rhythm work stays energetic and drives the band forward.
band_performance.Mickey: Pounds with power, delivering crisp drum tones that sit perfectly in the mix.
band_performance.Billy: Kit sits cleanly in the mix, offering tight, precise drumming that complements Mickey’s groove.
